Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mildly elevated HbA1c, suggesting prediabetes, and high hemoglobin/hematocrit, which may indicate dehydration or polycythemia and should be clinically correlated. Chloride is slightly elevated and CO₂ is low, possibly reflecting mild metabolic acidosis. Total cholesterol is low, but lipid fractions are within range. Other parameters are unremarkable. Clinical correlation and follow-up are recommended, especially regarding glycemic control and erythrocytosis.

text.div: Provider Organization: Ospedale Civile di Voghera
